originalTitle: Russia agrees to stop using Kenyan recruits in Ukraine conflict, Kenya says
title: Russia agrees to stop using Kenyan recruits in Ukraine conflict, Kenya says
content: Some 1,000 Kenyans are believed to have recruited to fight in Ukraine - many say they were lured under false pretences.

originalTitle: Whistleblower murder suspect is former elite officer, South African police say
title: A Sad Story About a Brave Man in South Africa
content:
Hello from Sunny South Africa!

Hello, amazing friends! We're here in beautiful South Africa, a land full of sunshine, amazing animals like lions and elephants, and friendly people. Today, we have a story to share with you. It's a bit of a sad story, but it's also about being brave and making sure things are fair.

Meet Marius: A Man Who Cared

There was a kind man named Marius van der Merwe. Marius was a grown-up who loved his country. He cared a lot about making sure things were right and fair for everyone in South Africa. He was like a good neighbour who wants to make sure everyone in the street is safe and happy.

What Did Marius See?

One day, Marius saw something that he thought was not right. He believed some police officers might have done something wrong. He thought they were not following the rules when they were trying to catch someone who had done a robbery. A robbery is when someone takes things that don't belong to them, which is against the rules. Did you know? Police officers in South Africa wear special uniforms to show they are helpers! They often drive cars with blue lights and sirens, which they use to get to emergencies quickly.

Marius Spoke Up

Marius felt it was important to tell someone about what he saw. This is like when you see someone break a rule at school, and you tell your teacher so they can make things fair again. When a grown-up sees something wrong and tells the right people – like other grown-ups who can help fix it – we call them a 'whistleblower'. They 'blow the whistle' on something bad, just like a referee in a sports game! So, Marius told the grown-ups in charge, the people who look after the police. He wanted them to check and see if everything was okay. He wanted to make sure that everyone, even police officers, always follows the rules.

A Sad Day for Marius

Sadly, after Marius told people what he saw, something very sad happened. Three weeks later, Marius was shot. He passed away. This made many people very, very sad. It’s never okay for anyone to hurt another person. Everyone deserves to be safe.

Who Helps When Things Go Wrong?

When something bad like this happens, like someone getting hurt, we need special people to find out what happened. In South Africa, the police are the ones who help us find out. They are like detectives! They look for clues, talk to people, and try to understand everything. In this story, the police themselves are helping to find out who hurt Marius. They said that a grown-up they think might have hurt Marius used to be a very important police officer. This means the police are even looking at one of their own people to make sure everyone follows the rules. Did you know? South Africa has 11 official languages! This means many people speak different languages, but they all work together to make their country a better place.

Why Are Rules So Important?

Rules are super important for everyone! They help us stay safe, play fair, and know what to do. At home, you have rules like 'no running inside'. At school, you have rules like 'listen to your teacher'. These rules help make sure everyone is happy and safe. Police officers also have very important rules to follow. These rules help them do their job well and fairly. When a police officer helps catch a bad person, they must follow rules so that everything is done correctly and safely for everyone.

Being Brave and Trusting Grown-Ups

Marius was brave because he spoke up when he saw something he thought was wrong. It's always good to be brave and tell a trusted grown-up – like your mom, dad, teacher, or another family member – if you see something that makes you feel unsafe or sad. They can help you! Did you know? The police in South Africa have a special phone number you can call for emergencies, just like in many other countries. It's 10111, and you call it when you need urgent help!

Working Towards a Fair World

Even though this is a sad story, it reminds us that many people are working hard to make South Africa a fair and safe place. The police are trying their best to find out what happened to Marius and make sure justice is done. This means making sure the right person is found and things are made fair again. It's a big job, but with good people like Marius who are brave, and with police who follow the rules, we can all help make our world a better place. Let's remember to be kind, follow the rules, and always speak up to a trusted grown-up if we see something that isn't right. Everyone deserves to be safe and treated fairly!
summary: A kind man named Marius in South Africa was shot and passed away after he bravely told people he thought some police officers had broken rules, and now the police are working to find out who hurt him.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that it's important to be brave and speak up to trusted grown-ups when we see something wrong, and that everyone, even police, must follow rules to keep our country fair and safe for all.
